  • P102 — CAMERA: Adapting to Semantic Camouflage in Unsupervised Text-Attributed Graph Fraud Detection [#84, Innovative]
  • P120 — TERGAD: Structure-Aware Text-Enhanced Representations for Graph Anomaly Detection [#139, Moderately Innovative]

Model Compression & Parameter Efficiency — avg: 63 (Innovative)

P094 (SkillWeave) compresses domain-specific delta modules via joint weight-and-activation quantization (SkillZip). P130 (AgentDoG) trains 0.8B–8B guardrail models with taxonomy-guided data and influence-function purification. P137 quantifies LoRA memorization as a power-law function of rank and sequence length. P142 (UniSteer) learns a single universal velocity field for LLM activation steering across arbitrary text conditions. These papers demonstrate parameter efficiency through modular composition, learned sparsity, and compact parameterizations.
  • P094 — Skill Weaving: Efficient LLM Improvement via Modular Skillpacks [#30, Innovative]
  • P137 — How LoRA Remembers? A Parametric Memory Law for LLM Finetuning [#64, Innovative]
  • P130 — AgentDoG 1.5: A Lightweight and Scalable Alignment Framework for AI Agent Safety and Security [#104, Innovative]
  • P142 — UniSteer: Text-Guided Flow Matching in Activation Space for Versatile LLM Steering [#132, Moderately Innovative]

Agent Systems: Reinforcement Learning & Training — avg: 63 (Innovative)

This group addresses agent training via reinforcement learning and policy optimization. P003 (dGRPO) augments on-policy GRPO with dense token-level teacher guidance. P008 (SSOPD) converts intra-group correct/wrong contrasts in GRPO into dense prefix-level supervision. P009 (METIS) trains the RFT policy itself to predict prompt informativeness and calibrate rewards. P104 (Selective Rollout) terminates GRPO groups early when trajectories converge. P145 (AXPO) recovers learning signal on tool calls via resampling from truncated incorrect prefixes. P146 (DenoiseRL) augments rollouts with denoise continuations from weak models. P167 (DVAO) applies dynamic per-objective weighting in multi-reward GRPO. Together, they demonstrate dense supervision, trajectory reuse, and adaptive weighting in agent RL.
  • P104 — Selective Rollout: Mid-Trajectory Termination for Multi-Sample Agent RL [#49, Innovative]
  • P003 — Combining On-Policy Optimization and Distillation for Long-Context Reasoning in Large Language Models [#67, Innovative]
  • P145 — Agent Explorative Policy Optimization for Multimodal Agentic Reasoning [#68, Innovative]
  • P008 — Self-Supervised On-Policy Distillation for Reasoning Language Models [#85, Innovative]
  • P009 — Internalizing Curriculum Judgment for LLM Reinforcement Fine-Tuning [#91, Innovative]
  • P167 — DVAO: Dynamic Variance-adaptive Advantage Optimization for Multi-reward Reinforcement Learning [#100, Innovative]
  • P146 — DenoiseRL: Bootstrapping Reasoning Models to Recover from Noisy Prefixes [#129, Moderately Innovative]

Specialized Domains: Robotics & Embodied AI — avg: 63 (Innovative)

P014 (IndusAgent) fine-tunes Qwen3-VL-8B with SFT+GRPO for industrial anomaly detection via dynamic tool calling. P132 (Qwen-VLA) handles robot manipulation, vision-language navigation, and egocentric action prediction on a unified 8B backbone. P150 (GEM) adds flow-matching depth prediction as auxiliary task during VLM pre-training for spatial grounding. These papers extend MLLMs and agents to embodied control and perception tasks.
  • P132 — Qwen-VLA: Unifying Vision-Language-Action Modeling across Tasks, Environments, and Robot Embodiments [#45, Innovative]
  • P150 — GEM: Generative Supervision Helps Embodied Intelligence [#80, Innovative]
  • P014 — IndusAgent: Reinforcing Open-Vocabulary Industrial Anomaly Detection with Agentic Tools [#130, Moderately Innovative]

Specialized Domains: Chemistry & Biology — avg: 62 (Innovative)

P064 (SMDD-Bench) benchmarks agentic LLMs on 502 drug design tasks (2D pharmacophore, scaffolding, lead optimization). P100 overexpresses CHO genes to redirect metabolic flux and enhance IgG1 production. P122 reviews classical (VCA, CPA, SQS) and AI methods (MLIPs, GNNs) for chemical disorder modeling. P129 surveys reaction outcome prediction via data-driven and physics-based paradigms. These papers show LLMs and ML scaling to complex chemistry/biology domains with domain-specific benchmarks and classical method integration.
  • P064 — SMDD-Bench: Can LLMs Solve Real-World Small Molecule Drug Design Tasks? [#24, Innovative]
  • P129 — An overview of reaction outcome prediction with physics-based and data-driven methods [#56, Innovative]
  • P122 — Atomistic Modeling of Chemical Disorder in Materials: Bridging Classical Methods and AI-Assisted Approaches [#102, Innovative]
  • P100 — Gene overexpression reduces inhibitory metabolites to enhance CHO cell growth and IgG1 production [#148, Moderately Innovative]

Vision-Language Models: Fine-Grained Grounding — avg: 62 (Innovative)

This cluster focuses on precise object grounding and spatial understanding in VLMs. P038 recursively partitions large label sets to recover accuracy in closed-set recognition. P116 (PStar) retrieves difficulty-matched pseudocode paths to guide structured reasoning. P140 exposes and quantifies vertical-distance entanglement in VLM representations (conflating 2D vertical position with 3D depth). P156 (LocateAnything) decodes bounding boxes as atomic multi-token units in parallel rather than sequentially. P187 aligns cross-attention maps with segmentation masks for fine-grained object grounding. P188 (ETCHR) generates intermediate visual-thought images during reasoning via question-conditioned editing. Together they show VLMs are moving toward explicit spatial grounding, structured intermediate representations, and per-token calibration.
  • P156 — LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding [#20, Innovative]
  • P140 — Why Far Looks Up: Probing Spatial Representation in Vision-Language Models [#59, Innovative]
  • P188 — ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning [#83, Innovative]
  • P038 — Divide-and-Conquer Inference for Large-Scale Visual Recognition with Multimodal Large Language Models [#96, Innovative]
  • P187 — See What I Mean: Aligning Vision and Language Representations for Video Fine-grained Object Understanding [#108, Moderately Innovative]
  • P116 — Pseudocode-Guided Structured Reasoning for Automating Reliable Inference in Vision-Language Models [#147, Moderately Innovative]

LLM Inference: Serving & Performance Modeling — avg: 62 (Innovative)

These papers address measurement, simulation, and characterization of LLM inference at scale. P036 introduces Kavier, a discrete-event simulator for KV-cache-aware inference ecosystems. P042 exposes measurement bias in asyncio-based benchmarks at production scale, showing up to 16% metric inflation. P105 demonstrates that inference backend choice (vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp) acts as an undocumented hyperparameter. P127 extends simulation to disaggregated architectures and speculative decoding. P128 characterizes bottleneck shifts across model sizes and parallelism strategies. Together, they underscore the gap between lab benchmarks and production reality, and the need for backend-aware, large-scale simulation.
  • P105 — The Silent Hyperparameter: Quantifying the Impact of Inference Backends on LLM Reproducibility [#33, Innovative]
  • P127 — Frontier: Towards Comprehensive and Accurate LLM Inference Simulation [#41, Innovative]
  • P042 — Identifying and Mitigating Systemic Measurement Bias in Production LLM Inference Benchmarks [#77, Innovative]
  • P128 — Understanding Inference Scaling for LLMs: Bottlenecks, Trade-offs, and Performance Principles [#128, Moderately Innovative]
  • P036 — Kavier: Exploring Performance, Sustainability, and Efficiency of LLM Ecosystems under Inference through Cache-Aware Discrete-Event Simulation [#145, Moderately Innovative]

Video: Interactive & Long-Form Evaluation — avg: 60 (Innovative)

P072 converts diffusion models into interactive streaming generators via asymmetric attention. P158 (LongAV-Compass) benchmarks minute-scale audio-visual generation across T2AV/I2AV/V2AV. P166 (WBENCH) evaluates interactive world models on video quality, setting adherence, interaction adherence, consistency, and physics. P185 (VGenST-Bench) evaluates spatio-temporal reasoning via 1,200 synthetically generated videos. These papers establish evaluation and interactivity as core video model concerns.
  • P072 — Live Music Diffusion Models: Efficient Fine-Tuning and Post-Training of Interactive Diffusion Music Generators [#51, Innovative]
  • P166 — WBENCH: A Comprehensive Multi-turn Benchmark for Interactive Video World Model Evaluation [#74, Innovative]
  • P185 — VGenST-Bench: A Benchmark for Spatio-Temporal Reasoning via Active Video Synthesis [#79, Innovative]
  • P158 — LongAV-Compass: Towards Unified Evaluation of Minute-Scale Audio-Visual Generation Across T2AV, I2AV, and V2AV [#153, Moderately Innovative]

Vision-Language Models: Core Architecture & Reasoning — avg: 60 (Innovative)

These papers advance VLM architecture and reasoning capabilities. P013 interleaves explicit chain-of-thought with continuous audio-visual latent states. P020 uses entropy-trajectory penalties to suppress language shortcuts and late-stage instability in spatial reasoning. P024 combines dual semantic-spatial contrastive learning with decoupled two-stage VLM fine-tuning. P076 (OmniBoost) curates visually debiased evaluation data and applies staged post-training. P152 (NEO-ov) replaces encoder-dependent architectures with a monolithic autoregressive backbone processing raw pixels. P163 replaces uniform frame sampling with codec-stream tokenization (H.264/H.265 bit-cost routing). The trend shows movement toward unified architectures, adaptive representations, and hybrid supervision.
  • P163 — LLaVA-OneVision-2: Towards Next-Generation Perceptual Intelligence [#58, Innovative]
  • P020 — ProSR: Process-Shaped Spatial Reasoning for Reliable Chain-of-Thought in VLMs [#78, Innovative]
  • P152 — From Pixels to Words – Towards Native One-Vision Models at Scale [#95, Innovative]
  • P076 — Boosting Omni-Modal Language Models: Staged Post-Training with Visually Debiased Evaluation [#103, Innovative]
  • P013 — LatentOmni: Rethinking Omni-Modal Understanding via Unified Audio-Visual Latent Reasoning [#119, Moderately Innovative]
  • P024 — VAMER: Visual-Anchored Multimodal Evidence Reasoning for Knowledge-Based VQA [#138, Moderately Innovative]

Efficiency, Scaling & Resource Optimization — avg: 60 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers address efficiency-capacity tradeoffs from complementary angles. P190 unifies scaling law formulations under a Shannon-theoretic framework capturing both monotonic improvement and U-shaped degradation regimes, enabling better prediction of capacity under quantization and overtraining. P103 leverages model-specific compute-communication ratios—particularly the communication-bound nature of MoE architectures—to jointly optimize GPU power caps and batch size via closed-loop control, achieving 1.26× efficiency gains while reducing QoS violations 4–7×. P058 applies domain-adaptive continued pretraining to build a Hebrew-specialized model activating only ~3B of 30B parameters per forward pass, delivering 9× throughput while maintaining competitive accuracy. The progression from P190's theoretical characterization of scaling limits, through P103's system-level resource management, to P058's practical language-specialized deployment demonstrates that efficiency gains accumulate across formal understanding of capacity constraints, intelligent hardware allocation, and architecture selection.
  • P190 — LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws [#88, Innovative]
  • P103 — PALS: Power-Aware LLM Serving for Mixture-of-Experts Models [#107, Moderately Innovative]
  • P058 — HEBATRON: A Hebrew-Specialized Open-Weight Mixture-of-Experts Language Model [#122, Moderately Innovative]

Graph Neural Networks: Architecture & Theory — avg: 60 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster addresses GNN design, optimization, and theoretical understanding. P019 (S³GNN) combines low-rank global mixing with sparse message passing to mitigate oversquashing. P079 proves mini-batch SGD on GNNs implicitly minimizes epoch-averaged loss plus gradient-variance regularization. P081 replaces eigenvalue computation with a neural approximation for spectral bisection. P089 (MDL-GBTRSC) regularizes sample-level affinities with MDL-guided granular-ball trees. P090 surveys 63 MLN structure learning algorithms. P115 (AxonCUDA) enforces both partition size and distinct hyperedge constraints in GPU-parallel hypergraph partitioning. P118 (FlareDTDG) applies full-batch to recent snapshots and coarser sampling to older ones for dynamic graph training. Together, they show GNNs maturing toward explicit oversquashing mitigation, implicit regularization understanding, and structure-preserving computation.
  • P079 — Implicit Regularization of Mini-Batch Training in Graph Neural Networks [#3, Highly Innovative]
  • P115 — Hypergraph Partitioning on GPU with Distinct Incident Hyperedges and Size Constraints [#4, Highly Innovative]
  • P118 — FlareDTDG: Harnessing Temporal Recency for Scalable Discrete-Time Dynamic Graph Training [#5, Highly Innovative]
  • P019 — S3GNN: Efficient Global Mixing and Local Message Passing for Long-Range Graph Learning [#12, Highly Innovative]
  • P089 — Minimum Description Length based Granular-Ball Tree Regularization for Spectral Clustering [#141, Moderately Innovative]
  • P090 — A Review of Structure Learning in Markov Logic Network [#177, Incremental]
  • P081 — Neural Acceleration for Graph Partitioning [#179, Incremental]

Research Automation & Scientific Discovery — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

P062 (dissertation) introduces Dynamic Evidence Trees, a metadata-aware RAG framework, and DataWeave for analytics over structured information. P157 (ScientistOne) maintains typed evidence chains throughout research (literature → solution → paper writing) with a CoE verification framework. P148 (RESEARCHMATH) extracts 14K research-level math problems and generates 220K reasoning trajectories via multi-agent pipelines. All three demonstrate systematic, evidence-grounded approaches to automating research and scientific discovery.
  • P148 — RESEARCHMATH-14K: Scaling Research-Level Mathematics via Agents [#93, Innovative]
  • P157 — ScientistOne: Towards Human-Level Autonomous Research via Chain-of-Evidence [#101, Innovative]
  • P062 — Improving LLM Reasoning and Retrieval for Structured and Complex Information Spaces [#140, Moderately Innovative]

Retrieval, Search & Information Discovery — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

P067 (Structured Semantic Search) augments semantic retrieval with table discovery operators (unionability, joinability). P074 evaluates source attribution in research agents via deterministic AST parsing and topical relevance. P176 (SMART) converts single-vector embeddings into multi-vector retrievers via MAXSIM late-interaction. These papers advance retrieval toward structured table discovery, verifiable attribution, and richer interaction patterns.
  • P176 — Your Embedding Model is SMARTer Than You Think [#37, Innovative]
  • P074 — Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents [#116, Moderately Innovative]
  • P067 — Diversed Model Discovery via Structured Table Discovery [#155, Moderately Innovative]

Multimodal & Cross-Lingual Integration — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers tackle combining information across modalities and languages within unified generative systems. P135 uses executable code (SVG, HTML, Three.js) as a deterministic structural intermediate between LLM reasoning and pixel-space image generation, sidestepping the expressiveness mismatch of natural language for spatial specification—achieving 0.925 accuracy on object-count tasks versus 0.85 for prompt-only baselines. P171 synthesizes 40+ multimodal models into a formal taxonomy organized by fusion depth and modality flow, extracting regime-specific engineering prescriptions (differential learning rates for mid-fusion, QK-Norm stabilization for early-fusion) that decode why architectural choices constrain training recipes. P057 addresses cross-lingual semantic alignment in topic models using LLM-based self-consistent word refinement and multilingual embeddings to enforce coherence across languages, gaining up to 51.2% on cross-lingual topic coherence. A unifying theme is that coordination across modalities or languages requires explicit intermediate representations—code, latent fusion boundaries, or semantic-space alignment—rather than naive end-to-end training.
  • P171 — Toward Native Multimodal Modeling: A Roadmap [#106, Innovative]
  • P057 — LLM-XTM: Enhancing Cross-Lingual Topic Models with Large Language Models [#117, Moderately Innovative]
  • P135 — GenClaw: Code-Driven Agentic Image Generation [#126, Moderately Innovative]

Safety, Reliability & System Behavior — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers collectively address understanding, detecting, and monitoring failure modes in deployed AI systems. P035 frames the problem at the model architecture level—showing how interpretability mechanisms themselves introduce adversarial vulnerabilities in Concept Bottleneck Models—while P051 tackles post-hoc hallucination detection via token-level classifiers trained on LLM output trajectories. P165 complements this by exploiting the unique multi-step denoising trajectory of diffusion LLMs to route safety monitoring dynamically, achieving 95% of oracle efficiency. P153 takes a meta-analytical perspective, demonstrating that AI research agents systematically concentrate around existing literature rather than exploring unconventional directions—a distributional bias with downstream impact on research agendas. Together, they suggest complementary approaches: architectural hardening (P035), per-token monitoring (P051), trajectory-aware routing (P165), and meta-level behavior characterization (P153). The trend indicates that safety and reliability are deeply intertwined with model architecture, inference dynamics, and aggregate behavioral patterns rather than post-hoc patches.
  • P165 — D²-Monitor: Dynamic Safety Monitoring for Diffusion LLMs via Hesitation-Aware Routing [#75, Innovative]
  • P051 — Scalable Token-Level Hallucination Detection in Large Language Models [#81, Innovative]
  • P153 — AI Research Agents Narrow Scientific Exploration [#105, Innovative]
  • P035 — When Interpretability Becomes a Liability: Adversarial Attacks on CBM Concept Layers [#165, Moderately Innovative]

Video Generation & Synthesis: Core Models — avg: 56 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster addresses video generation at scale and quality. P131 (minWM) converts video diffusion models into interactive few-step autoregressive world models. P136 (YoCausal) evaluates causal cognition via reversed videos as zero-cost counterfactuals. P144 (Gamma-World) generates synchronized multi-agent video via Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding and Sparse Hub Attention. P154 (EvalVerse) provides 45 cinematic evaluation dimensions with expert calibration. P161 (Soap2Soap) remakes full TV episodes while preserving narrative structure and character identity. P175 (Pantheon360) generates trajectory-controlled 360° video via 3D point-cloud caching. The trend shows movement toward interactive generation, multi-agent scenes, and verifiable cinematic quality.
  • P136 — YoCausal: How Far is Video Generation from World Model? A Causality Perspective [#61, Innovative]
  • P144 — Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players [#62, Innovative]
  • P175 — Pantheon360: Taming Digital Twin Generation via 3D-Aware 360° Video Diffusion [#94, Innovative]
  • P154 — EvalVerse: Pipeline-Aware and Expert-Calibrated Benchmarking for Professional Cinematic Video Generation [#136, Moderately Innovative]
  • P161 — Soap2Soap: Long Cinematic Video Remaking via Multi-Agent Collaboration [#142, Moderately Innovative]
  • P131 — minWM: A Full-Stack Open-Source Framework for Real-Time Interactive Video World Models [#170, Moderately Innovative]

LLM Inference: Hardware Acceleration & Compilation — avg: 56 (Moderately Innovative)

This group addresses hardware-level acceleration and compilation for inference. P119 characterizes decode throughput degradation under partial CPU-GPU offloading and job preemption costs. P121 (TIDE) exploits temporal stability of MoE expert activations to avoid redundant migrations. P124 benchmarks four ML compiler workflows (Inductor, XLA, TensorRT) on RTX 4090/5090. P125 introduces LlamaWeb, enabling GPU-accelerated inference in browsers via WebGPU. P126 presents operational lessons from OCR+LLM microservice pipelines at scale. The trend shows movement toward compiler-aware optimization, cross-platform portability, and production-grade system design.
  • P125 — Llamas on the Web: Memory-Efficient, Performance-Portable, and Multi-Precision LLM Inference with WebGPU [#11, Highly Innovative]
  • P121 — TIDE: Efficient and Lossless MoE Diffusion LLM Inference with I/O-aware Expert Offload [#71, Innovative]
  • P119 — Towards Multi-Model LLM Schedulers: Empirical Insights into Offloading and Preemption [#131, Moderately Innovative]
  • P124 — Characterization of machine learning compilers for LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs [#151, Moderately Innovative]
  • P126 — Operationalizing Document AI: A Microservice Architecture for OCR and LLM Pipelines in Production [#159, Moderately Innovative]

LLM Training: Safety & Alignment — avg: 56 (Moderately Innovative)

P011 compares four alignment-removal interventions on small models for security research. P012 (S²R²) decomposes outputs into semantic segments and penalizes worst-drift segments during LoRA fine-tuning. P049 (OPSA) trains on the model's own rollouts with dense KL divergence from a privileged-context teacher copy. These papers address the safety-reasoning tradeoff through on-policy distillation and robust adapter tuning.
  • P049 — Reducing the Safety Tax in LLM Safety Alignment with On-Policy Self-Distillation [#63, Innovative]
  • P011 — Ablating Safety: Mechanisms for Removing Alignment in Language Models for Security Applications [#125, Moderately Innovative]
  • P012 — Where Do Prompt Perturbations Break Generation? A Segment-Level View of Robustness in LoRA-Tuned Language Models [#154, Moderately Innovative]

Privacy, Security & Adversarial Robustness — avg: 55 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster explores privacy, security, and adversarial robustness across complementary and competing fronts. P010 and P022 develop defensive mechanisms for sensitive data, with P010 synthesizing private relational data via an adapted PATE ensemble and differential privacy, while P022 optimizes the computational cost of differential privacy training through randomized gradient clipping. P044 proposes a quantum-inspired federated learning framework for medical settings, claiming to break the classical privacy-utility trade-off through entanglement-based noise generation. In contrast, P046 and P053 investigate the adversarial side of privacy and security, with P046 systematically circumventing LLM safety mechanisms through iterative prompt reframing and semantic filtering, and P053 extracting voting secrets from webcam video via eye-movement analysis. Together, these papers illustrate a field defined by escalating offense-defense cycles: privacy-preserving techniques (P010, P022, P044) are continually matched by increasingly sophisticated attacks (P046, P053), suggesting that true privacy and security require ongoing co-evolution of both protective and adversarial methods.
  • P053 — Your Eyes Won't Lie: Snooping Online Voting Privacy from User Webcam [#17, Innovative]
  • P010 — Prism: Private Relational Data Synthesis with Language Models [#23, Innovative]
  • P022 — Efficient DP-SGD for LLMs with Randomized Clipping [#44, Innovative]
  • P046 — Adversarial Reframing: A Framework for Targeted Generation in Language Models [#160, Moderately Innovative]
  • P044 — A Neuro-Cognitive Quantum Architecture for Trustworthy AI in Ultra-Scarce Medical Environments [#184, Routine]

Agent Systems: Architecture & Frameworks — avg: 55 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers establish foundational agent architectures and frameworks. P059 frames harness engineering as joint optimization of workflows, memory, skill libraries, and multi-agent orchestration. P082 compiles agentic workflows into model weights, achieving frontier quality at 1/100 cost. P168 (Foundation Protocol) unifies identity, governance, and economic primitives for multi-agent coordination. P169 (Macaron-A2UI) generates executable UI actions via LoRA+GRPO. P172 (AutoResearch) defines an L0–L4 autonomy spectrum for AI research automation. P174 (MiniMax-M2) demonstrates agent-driven data pipelines grounded in executable workspaces. P189 (Quest) trains deep research agents via synthetic hierarchical rubric trees. The trend emphasizes infrastructure, cost-efficiency, and verifiable reasoning through structured intermediate representations.
  • P189 — Quest: Training Frontier Deep Research Agents with Fully Synthetic Tasks [#39, Innovative]
  • P169 — Macaron-A2UI: A Model for Generative UI in Personal Agents [#99, Innovative]
  • P082 — Compiling Agentic Workflows into LLM Weights: Near-Frontier Quality at Two Orders of Magnitude Less Cost [#112, Moderately Innovative]
  • P174 — The MiniMax-M2 Series: Mini Activations Unleashing Max Real-World Intelligence [#113, Moderately Innovative]
  • P172 — AutoResearch AI: Towards AI-Powered Research Automation for Scientific Discovery [#135, Moderately Innovative]
  • P059 — Agent Systems with Harness Engineering [#152, Moderately Innovative]
  • P168 — Foundation Protocol: A Coordination Layer for Agentic Society [#163, Moderately Innovative]

Hardware & Distributed Systems — avg: 55 (Moderately Innovative)

P029 surveys domain decomposition methods (Schwarz, GenEO, DtN). P032 (BACE-Pipe) minimizes JCT and electricity cost via geo-distributed pipeline scheduling. P047 surveys energy-aware computing across Cloud-Edge-HPC. P061 measures SeisSol parallel performance on RISC-V vs. x86. P078 introduces MPI+Kokkos GCP decomposition and federated asynchronous variants. The trend shows movement toward energy-efficiency, geo-distribution, and heterogeneous hardware support.
  • P029 — A Guided Tour of Modern Domain Decomposition: From Schwarz Iterations to Robust Preconditioners and HPC Implementations [#53, Innovative]
  • P078 — Synchronous and Asynchronous Parallelism Approaches for Generalized Canonical Polyadic Tensor Decomposition with GenTen [#54, Innovative]
  • P061 — Performance Analysis of Scientific Applications on Multiple RISC-V Platforms [#121, Moderately Innovative]
  • P047 — Energy-Aware Computing in the Year 2026 [#164, Moderately Innovative]
  • P032 — Bandwidth-Aware and Cost-Efficient Pipeline Parallel Scheduling in Geo-Distributed LLM Training [#167, Moderately Innovative]

Knowledge Graphs: Retrieval & Discovery — avg: 55 (Moderately Innovative)

P138 (OmniRetrieval) routes natural-language queries to heterogeneous sources (text, SQL, SPARQL) and dispatches native queries, unifying access across modalities. P179 (SciAtlas) constructs a 43M-paper academic KG with 3B triplets and a neuro-symbolic tri-path retrieval algorithm. Both demonstrate that KGs enable efficient discovery and reasoning across large, heterogeneous scholarly knowledge, with P179 showing how structured extraction scales to scientific literature.
  • P138 — OmniRetrieval: Unified Retrieval across Heterogeneous Knowledge Sources [#47, Innovative]
  • P179 — SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research [#162, Moderately Innovative]

Knowledge Graph Construction & Schema Inference — avg: 54 (Moderately Innovative)

This group addresses systematic KG construction from diverse sources. P007 combines fine-tuned BART with GNNs for intra-row semantics and relational context in databases. P028 introduces GeSI and EmSI for deriving conceptual schemas (entities, hierarchies, relationships) from raw tabular data. P045 argues for hybrid architectures combining symbolic ontologies, LLM-driven extraction, and human validation. P086 (KGI-Bench) evaluates end-to-end KG integration pipelines with a movie dataset (RDF/JSON/text). P113 (BLINKG) benchmarks LLM-generated declarative semantic mappings from heterogeneous sources to ontologies. The trend favors end-to-end evaluation, hybrid automation, and support for multiple data formats and ontology languages.
  • P028 — Conceptual Schema Inference for Tabular Datasets using Large Language Models [#15, Innovative]
  • P113 — BLINKG: A Benchmark for LLM-Integrated Knowledge Graph Generation [#38, Innovative]
  • P086 — Evaluation of Pipelines for Data Integration into Knowledge Graphs [#72, Innovative]
  • P007 — Towards Foundation Models for Relational Databases with Language Models and Graph Neural Networks [#133, Moderately Innovative]
  • P045 — Improving Biomedical Knowledge Graph Construction through Large Language Model Driven Literature Mining and Semantic Relationship Extraction [#186, Routine]

Multimodal: Audio & Speech Integration — avg: 54 (Moderately Innovative)

P073 surveys Large Audio Language Models with a six-pillar trustworthiness taxonomy (2022–2026). P181 (StepAudio 2.5) unifies ASR, TTS, and realtime dialogue on a shared MoE-LLM backbone with task-tailored RLHF. Both demonstrate audio-language integration moving from separate pipelines to unified foundation models with domain-specific specialization.
  • P073 — A Survey of Large Audio Language Models: Generalization, Trustworthiness, and Outlook [#115, Moderately Innovative]
  • P181 — StepAudio 2.5 Technical Report [#143, Moderately Innovative]

LLM Inference: Quantization & Precision — avg: 54 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster covers precision and quantization strategies for efficient inference. P015 applies phase-aware quantization (W4A4 prefill, BF16 decode), while P030 routes requests across five quantized/unquantized modes based on workload. P069 introduces RISC-V ISA extensions for rotation-based quantization, and P096 targets mobile NPUs with static-only quantization. P123 resolves structural mismatch between sparse attention and dynamic quantization, while P178 uses selective mixed-precision (FP16 for top-k, FP4 for remainder). The trend reflects movement from uniform to adaptive, per-operation, and hardware-aware quantization schemes.
  • P096 — Quant.npu: Enabling Efficient Mobile NPU Inference for on-device LLMs via Fully Static Quantization [#70, Innovative]
  • P178 — ThriftAttention: Selective Mixed Precision for Long-Context FP4 Attention [#82, Innovative]
  • P015 — Mix-Quant: Quantized Prefilling, Precise Decoding for Agentic LLMs [#127, Moderately Innovative]
  • P123 — SSV: Sparse Speculative Verification for Efficient LLM Inference [#137, Moderately Innovative]
  • P069 — FWHT-RVV: A RISC-V vector processor with FWHT instruction extension for edge LLM inference [#149, Moderately Innovative]
  • P030 — ModeSwitch-LLM: A Lightweight Phase-Aware Controller for Cross-Mode LLM Inference on a Single GPU [#161, Moderately Innovative]

Time Series & Temporal Forecasting — avg: 53 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster spans time series modeling and anomaly detection. P016 (Toto 2.0) introduces five open-weights time series foundation models (4m–2.5B) demonstrating monotonic improvement with scale. P023 (ContrastAD) treats inter-variable graph divergence as the primary anomaly signal via soft contrastive regularization. P056 surveys 150+ foundation models for structured data (tabular, time series, graphs). P065 (Chronicle) jointly trains on natural language and time series with shared transformer backbone. P077 evaluates Chronos-2 on multivariate financial forecasting. The trend shows movement toward scale-aware foundation models, graph-based anomaly signals, and unified multimodal representations.
  • P065 — Chronicle: A Multimodal Foundation Model for Joint Language and Time Series Understanding [#66, Innovative]
  • P016 — Toto 2.0: Time Series Forecasting Enters the Scaling Era [#73, Innovative]
  • P023 — Contrast to Detect: Dynamic Graph Contrastive Regularization for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series [#98, Innovative]
  • P056 — A Survey on Foundation Models for Structured Data: Tabular, Time Series, and Graphs [#173, Incremental]
  • P077 — Multivariate Financial Forecasting using the Chronos Time Series Foundation Models [#175, Incremental]

Recommendation Systems — avg: 50 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers collectively demonstrate the maturation of recommendation systems across diverse domains and constraints. P025 tackles decentralized multi-robot task allocation through noise-weighted collaborative filtering, while P026 develops theory for jointly optimizing communication cost and recommendation set size—together addressing scalability and efficiency at opposite ends of the architectural spectrum. P027 and P048 represent production-oriented approaches: P027 implements privacy-preserving federated ranking on mobile devices, whereas P048 proposes a comprehensive five-layer e-commerce architecture with drift detection. Beyond these foundational techniques, the cluster shows growing adoption of novel methodologies: P101 leverages large language models to enrich course recommendation embeddings, P141 applies reinforcement learning with gradient rectification for proactive recommendations, and P080 benchmarks both classical and deep-learning baselines on e-commerce data. P114 synthesizes multiple filtering paradigms (content, collaborative, NLP-based clustering, and decision trees) for the internship domain, exemplifying the trend toward hybrid, multi-technique systems. Collectively, these papers suggest the field is moving beyond single-algorithm approaches toward specialized architectures that balance accuracy, privacy, efficiency, and application-specific constraints.
  • P141 — ProRL: Effective Reinforcement Learning for Proactive Recommendation via Rectified Policy Gradient Estimation [#10, Highly Innovative]
  • P026 — Right-Sizing Communication and Recommendation Set Size in AI-Assisted Search [#19, Innovative]
  • P025 — Acting on the Unseen: Communication-Free Collaborative Filtering for Decentralized Multi-Robot Task Allocation [#32, Innovative]
  • P101 — LLM-CR: LLM-enhanced course recommendation [#123, Moderately Innovative]
  • P027 — Building a privacy-preserving Federated Recommender system for mobile devices [#158, Moderately Innovative]
  • P048 — Personalized Marketing Through Artificial Intelligence: Architectures, Techniques, and Emerging Frontiers [#166, Moderately Innovative]
  • P080 — Comparative Analysis of Traditional and Deep Learning Approaches for E-Commerce Product Recommendations: A Study on Amazon Dataset [#180, Incremental]
  • P114 — AI-Based Internship Recommendation Engine for PM Internship Scheme [#185, Routine]

Symbolic & Logical Reasoning in LLMs — avg: 49 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers hybridize neural models with formal logic and symbolic search. P039 uses an LLM to extract fuzzy predicates from clinical notes and a Prolog engine for auditable differential diagnosis. P092 derives Hebbian learning for n-th order synaptic connections to implement higher-order Horn clause logic. P093 (SSA) structurally enforces state-equivalence invariance in transformers trained on backtracking-search traces. P147 (BES) escapes autoregressive probability shells by recombining partial trajectories via evolution-inspired operators. Collectively, they demonstrate that formal reasoning and symbolic search can be either embedded in or augment neural models for interpretability and exploration.
  • P093 — Can Transformers Learn to Verify During Backtracking Search? [#1, Highly Innovative]
  • P147 — Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search [#60, Innovative]
  • P039 — Uncertainty Reasoning with Large Language Models for Explainable Disease Diagnosis [#172, Incremental]
  • P092 — Accurate Derivation and Efficient Implementation of Higher Order Logic Programming in Hopfield Network [#187, Routine]

Knowledge Tracing & Learning Analytics — avg: 44 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers advance student knowledge modeling and adaptive learning. P017 reviews AI-based diagnosis (descriptive study). P018 (StanBKT) enables full Bayesian inference for knowledge tracing via Stan. P021 introduces a benchmark for controllable student simulation via selective skill mastery. P040 (XAI/RAI review) synthesizes 128 studies on explainable and responsible AI in adaptive systems. P063 (DLAKT) tracks per-skill mastery and multidimensional domain ability with interpretable exponential growth. P095 (LOXER) provides a learner-centered ontology and explanation framework for lifelong learning. The trend shows movement toward Bayesian rigor, interpretability, and multidimensional ability models.
  • P018 — StanBKT: Rethinking Parameter Estimation in Bayesian Knowledge Tracing [#48, Innovative]
  • P095 — Knowledge-aware Methods for Explainable Decision Support in Lifelong Learning [#90, Innovative]
  • P021 — Toward a Benchmark for Controllable Simulation of Imperfect Students with Large Language Models [#110, Moderately Innovative]
  • P063 — Dynamic Domain Learning Ability Enhanced Knowledge Tracing with Stability [#134, Moderately Innovative]
  • P040 — Explainable and responsible artificial intelligence in education: Ethical challenges in adaptive learning systems [#183, Routine]
  • P017 — AI-Based Diagnostics and Prediction of Student Learning [#188, Routine]

Knowledge Graphs: Domain-Specific Applications — avg: 43 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster applies knowledge graphs to specialized domains. P109 (maPO) converts MAPF execution traces into RDF for querying and explaining planner behavior. P110 synthesizes heterogeneous diagnostic data (temporal, relational, file-based) for high-voltage equipment via ontology-driven warehouse design. P106 fuses Amharic domain ontologies with TF-IDF for news classification. P107 proposes extending the IDS-RAM Vocabulary Hub to catalog dataset profiles and alignment artifacts. These papers show KG techniques scaling to domain-specific provenance tracking, multilingual NLP, and cross-domain metadata harmonization.
  • P106 — Enhancing Amharic News Classification through Ontology-Based Feature Fusion and Logistic Regression [#97, Innovative]
  • P109 — maPO: An Ontology for Multi-Agent Path Finding and Its Usage for Explaining Planner Behaviour [#150, Moderately Innovative]
  • P107 — The Vocabulary Hub as a Catalog for Semantic Artifacts for Discovery and Alignment of Datasets [#157, Moderately Innovative]
  • P110 — Ontology-Based Methodology for the Structural Synthesis of a Heterogeneous Data Warehouse for Diagnostics of High-Voltage Equipment at Power Facilities [#181, Incremental]

Knowledge Graphs: Ontologies & Semantic Integration — avg: 38 (Incremental)

These papers leverage formal ontologies for semantic integration and reasoning. P043 pairs a 127-class OWL ontology with SWRL rules for organic certification recommendations. P052 uses a 16-class RDF ontology with SPARQL queries for heterogeneous student performance retrieval. P060 combines UML process models with OWL ontologies for blended learning. P087 applies TransD entity alignment to resolve semantic conflicts in earth observation networks. P088 characterizes the ontological continuum (semantics vs. pragmatics, properties vs. objects). P111 uses Claude 4.5 as a 'knowledge worker' to populate and validate OntoAgent ontologies. Together, they show ontologies as primary integration tools, with growing emphasis on automatic validation and LLM-assisted population.
  • P088 — Knowledge Graph Re-engineering Along the Ontological Continuum (Extended version) [#89, Innovative]
  • P052 — Advancing semantic search in higher education through the Heterogeneous Student Performance data retrieval model [#169, Moderately Innovative]
  • P087 — Semantic Conflict Resolution for Collaborative Earth Observation via Ontology Aware Entity Alignment [#171, Incremental]
  • P111 — LLMs as Knowledge Workers [#174, Incremental]
  • P043 — Ontology-Based Decision Support for Young Agripreneurs in Organic Agriculture Using Semantic Web Rule Language [#178, Incremental]
  • P060 — Two-Layer Ontology-Based Knowledge Framework for Blended Learning: A Case Study in Higher Education [#182, Incremental]

Domain-Specific Applications & Knowledge Systems — avg: 37 (Incremental)

These papers situate AI and LLM technology within specific organizational and domain contexts. P054 systematically reviews 183 academic papers and 35 industry reports on AI-ERP integration, finding that ethics/governance represents <2% of discourse despite ERP systems' handling of payroll, HR, and financial transactions—a credible structural blind spot. P108 applies DOLCE foundational ontology to formally model building elements as persistent entities with phase-dependent properties, enabling cross-file change tracking in BIM workflows and addressing the practical problem that IFC files destroy element identity continuity across project phases. P091 proposes an unbuilt architectural framework for real-time financial LLM-augmented reasoning via tiered edge-cloud inference, though it contains no implementation, empirical validation, or algorithmic specificity. Together, these papers span a maturity spectrum: P054 surveys existing work and identifies gaps, P108 proposes a formal solution to a concrete interoperability problem validated at toy scale, and P091 remains purely aspirational. The collective implication is that domain integration of AI requires systematic understanding of prior art, formal knowledge representation grounded in ontology, and—critically—validated implementation evidence.
  • P108 — Temporally-Qualified Building Elements: A DOLCE-Based Ontology for Phase-Dependent Identity and Change Tracking in BIM Models [#118, Moderately Innovative]
  • P054 — Unveiling AI-powered ERP systems: a systematic review of trends and future directions [#156, Moderately Innovative]
  • P091 — Fin-LLM-Inference: A High-Throughput Distributed System for Real-Time Financial Time Series Forecasting via Heterogeneous LLM-Augmented Reasoning Pipelines [#189, Routine]

Neuro-symbolic AI & Hybrid Reasoning — avg: 27 (Incremental)

These papers both explore hybrid neuro-symbolic architectures that merge neural networks with symbolic reasoning to enhance problem-solving capabilities. P034 takes a benchmarking approach, comparing a unified hybrid system against standalone neural and symbolic baselines across accuracy, interpretability, robustness, and generalization metrics, while P133 proposes a specialized instantiation of this paradigm for multimodal emotion recognition, integrating cross-modal attention encoders with Answer Set Programming constraints, Neural Turing Machines for episodic memory, and psychological scaffolding via the Hourglass of Emotions model. Where P034 emphasizes systematic evaluation across diverse datasets, P133 demonstrates domain-specific deepening through metacognitive uncertainty quantification and psychological grounding. Collectively, these works suggest a field trend toward architecting neural and symbolic components with domain expertise and metacognitive awareness to achieve interpretability and robustness in complex reasoning tasks.
  • P133 — CEREBRAL: A Neurosymbolic Framework for Multimodal Emotion Recognition with Psychological Constraints and Metacognitive Reasoning [#146, Moderately Innovative]
  • P034 — Hybrid Artificial Intelligence Models Combining Neural Networks and Symbolic Reasoning for Complex Problem Solving [#190, Routine]
